1	module EdgeCache
     2	  class BustArticle
     3	    TIMEFRAMES = [
     4	      [-> { 1.week.ago }, "week"],
     5	      [-> { 1.month.ago }, "month"],
     6	      [-> { 1.year.ago }, "year"],
     7	      [-> { 5.years.ago }, "infinity"],
     8	    ].freeze
     9	    def self.call(article)
    10	      return unless article
    11	
    12	      article.purge
    13	      article.user&.purge
    14	      article.organization&.purge
    15	
    16	      cache_bust = EdgeCache::Bust.new
    17	
    18	      bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
    19	      bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
    20	      bust_user_profile_pages(article)
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    def self.bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
    24	      if article.published_at.to_i > Time.current.to_i
    25	        # TODO
    26	        cache_bust.call("/")
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      if article.video.present? && article.published_at.to_i > 10.days.ago.to_i
    30	        cache_bust.call("/videos")
    31	      end
    32	
    33	      TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
    34	        next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call)
    35	          .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
    36	
    37	        cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      if article.published && article.published_at > 1.hour.ago
    41	        cache_bust.call("/latest")
    42	      end
    43	
    44	      cache_bust.call("/") if Article.published.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(4).ids.include?(article.id)
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    private_class_method :bust_home_pages
    48	
    49	    def self.bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
    50	      return unless article.published
    51	
    52	      article.tag_list.each do |tag|
    53	        if article.published_at.to_i > 2.minutes.ago.to_i
    54	          cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}/latest")
    55	        end
    56	
    57	        TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
    58	          next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call).cached_tagged_with_any(tag)
    59	            .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
    60	
    61	          cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
    62	          12.times do |i|
    63	            cache_bust.call("/api/articles?tag=#{tag}&top=#{i}")
    64	          end
    65	        end
    66	
    67	        next unless rand(2) == 1 &&
    68	          Article.published.cached_tagged_with(tag)
    69	            .order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(2).ids.include?(article.id)
    70	
    71	        cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}")
    72	      end
    73	    end
    74	
    75	    private_class_method :bust_tag_pages
    76	
    77	    def self.bust_user_profile_pages(article)
    78	      return unless article.user
    79	
    80	      EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
    81	        article.user.profile_cache_keys,
    82	        fallback_paths: article.user.profile_cache_bust_paths,
    83	      )
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    private_class_method :bust_user_profile_pages
    87	  end
    88	end

1	module Articles
     2	  # This module is responsible for updating a specific page view for a given article and user.
     3	  #
     4	  # @see Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker for the sibling that's responsible for recording page
     5	  #      views.
     6	  module PageViewUpdater
     7	    EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER = 60
     8	    # @param article_id [Integer]
     9	    # @param user_id [Integer]
    10	    #
    11	    # @return [TrueClass] if we updated or created a PageView
    12	    # @return [FalseClass] if we did not update a PageView
    13	    #
    14	    # @note Regardless of return status, consider the business logic successful unless we raise an
    15	    #       exception.  The return value is present for easing testing.  The `find_or_create_by`
    16	    #       adds a complication in the testing logic
    17	    #       (e.g., `expect { Articles::PageViewUpdater.call }.not_to change(PageView, :count) `
    18	    def self.call(article_id:, user_id:)
    19	      # Don't record views to non-existent or unpublished articles.
    20	      return false unless Article.published.exists?(id: article_id)
    21	      # Don't record author's own views.
    22	      return false if Article.published.from_subforem.exists?(id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    23	
    24	      page_view = PageView.order(created_at: :desc)
    25	        .find_or_create_by(article_id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    26	      return true if page_view.new_record?
    27	
    28	      new_time_mark = page_view.time_tracked_in_seconds + 15
    29	      page_view.update_column(:time_tracked_in_seconds, new_time_mark)
    30	      if new_time_mark == EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER
    31	        FeedEvent.record_journey_for(page_view.user, article: page_view.article, category: :extended_pageview)
    32	        UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(user_id, article_id, 0.05) if user_id
    33	      end
    34	
    35	      true
    36	    end
    37	  end
    38	end

1	class FeedEvent < ApplicationRecord
     2	  # These are "optional" mostly so that we can perform validated bulk inserts
     3	  # without triggering article/user validation.
     4	  # Since there are database-level constraints, it's fine to skip the automatic
     5	  # Rails-side association validation (which causes an N+1 query).
     6	  belongs_to :article, optional: true
     7	  belongs_to :user, optional: true
     8	  belongs_to :feed_config, optional: true
     9	
    10	  after_save :update_article_counters_and_scores
    11	  after_save :create_feed_config_offshoot
    12	  after_create_commit :record_field_test_event
    13	
    14	  enum :category, {
    15	    impression: 0,
    16	    click: 1,
    17	    reaction: 2,
    18	    comment: 3,
    19	    extended_pageview: 4
    20	  }
    21	
    22	  CONTEXT_TYPE_HOME = "home".freeze
    23	  CONTEXT_TYPE_SEARCH = "search".freeze
    24	  CONTEXT_TYPE_TAG = "tag".freeze
    25	  CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL = "email".freeze
    26	  CONTEXT_TYPE_SIDEBAR = "sidebar".freeze
    27	  VALID_CONTEXT_TYPES = [
    28	    CONTEXT_TYPE_HOME,
    29	    CONTEXT_TYPE_SEARCH,
    30	    CONTEXT_TYPE_TAG,
    31	    CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL,
    32	    CONTEXT_TYPE_SIDEBAR
    33	  ].freeze
    34	  DEFAULT_TIMEBOX = 5.minutes.freeze
    35	
    36	  REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER = 6
    37	  COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER = 12
    38	
    39	  validates :article_position, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than: 0 }
    40	  validates :context_type, inclusion: { in: VALID_CONTEXT_TYPES }, presence: true
    41	  # Since we have disabled association validation, this is handy to filter basic bad data
    42	  validates :article_id, presence: true, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    43	  validates :user_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }, allow_nil: true
    44	
    45	  def self.record_journey_for(user, article:, category:)
    46	    return unless %i[reaction comment extended_pageview].include?(category)
    47	
    48	    last_click = where(user: user, category: :click).last
    49	    return unless last_click&.article_id == article.id
    50	
    51	    create_with(last_click.slice(:article_position, :context_type, :feed_config_id))
    52	      .find_or_create_by(
    53	        category: category,
    54	        user: user,
    55	        article: article,
    56	      )
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  def self.bulk_update_counters_by_article_id(article_ids)
    60	    unique_article_ids = article_ids.uniq
    61	    update_counters_for_articles(unique_article_ids)
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def self.update_counters_for_articles(article_ids)
    65	    article_ids.each do |article_id|
    66	      update_single_article_counters(article_id)
    67	    end
    68	  end
    69	
    70	  def self.update_single_article_counters(article_id)
    71	    ThrottledCall.perform("article_feed_success_score_#{article_id}", throttle_for: 5.minutes) do
    72	      impressions = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "impression")
    73	      return if impressions.empty?
    74	
    75	      clicks = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "click")
    76	      reactions = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "reaction")
    77	      comments = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "comment")
    78	      pageviews = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "extended_pageview")
    79	
    80	      # Count the distinct users for impressions and each event type
    81	      distinct_impressions_users = impressions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    82	      distinct_clicks_users = clicks.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    83	      distinct_reactions_users = reactions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    84	      distinct_comments_users = comments.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    85	      distinct_pageviews_users = pageviews.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    86	
    87	      # Calculate score based on distinct users
    88	      reactions_score = distinct_reactions_users.size * REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER
    89	      clicks_score = distinct_clicks_users.size # 1x multiplier for clicks
    90	      comments_score = distinct_comments_users.size * COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER
    91	      pageviews_score = distinct_pageviews_users.size # 1x multiplier for extended pageviews
    92	
    93	      score = (clicks_score + pageviews_score + reactions_score + comments_score).to_f / distinct_impressions_users.size
    94	
    95	      # Update the article counters
    96	      Article.where(id: article_id).update_all(
    97	        feed_success_score: score,
    98	        feed_clicks_count: clicks.size,
    99	        feed_impressions_count: impressions.size,
   100	      )
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	
   104	  def self.update_single_feed_config_counters(feed_config_id)
   105	    ThrottledCall.perform("feed_config_feed_success_score_#{feed_config_id}", throttle_for: 5.minutes) do
   106	      impressions = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "impression")
   107	      return if impressions.empty?
   108	
   109	      clicks = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "click")
   110	      reactions = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "reaction")
   111	      comments = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "comment")
   112	      pageviews = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "extended_pageview")
   113	
   114	      # Count the distinct users for impressions and each event type
   115	      distinct_impressions_users = impressions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   116	      distinct_clicks_users = clicks.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   117	      distinct_reactions_users = reactions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   118	      distinct_comments_users = comments.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   119	      distinct_pageviews_users = pageviews.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   120	
   121	      # Calculate score based on distinct users
   122	      reactions_score = distinct_reactions_users.size * REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER * 2
   123	      clicks_score = clicks.sum("POWER(2.0/3, article_position - 1)")
   124	      comments_score = distinct_comments_users.size * COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER * 2
   125	      pageviews_score = distinct_pageviews_users.size # 1x multiplier for extended pageviews
   126	
   127	      score = (clicks_score + pageviews_score + reactions_score + comments_score).to_f / distinct_impressions_users.size
   128	      
   129	      # Update the feed config counters
   130	      FeedConfig.find_by(id: feed_config_id)&.update_columns(feed_success_score: score, feed_impressions_count: impressions.size)
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  private
   135	
   136	  def update_article_counters_and_scores
   137	    self.class.update_single_article_counters(article_id) if article_id
   138	    self.class.update_single_feed_config_counters(feed_config_id) if feed_config_id
   139	  end
   140	
   141	  def create_feed_config_offshoot
   142	    # Any time we get a reaction or comment, we branch off a slightly modified feed based on the success of the current one
   143	    return unless feed_config
   144	    return unless %w[reaction comment].include?(category)
   145	
   146	    feed_config.create_slightly_modified_clone!
   147	  end
   148	
   149	  # @see AbExperiment::GoalConversionHandler
   150	  def record_field_test_event
   151	    return if FieldTest.config["experiments"].nil?
   152	    return if category.to_s == "impression"
   153	    return unless user_id
   154	    return unless context_type == CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL # We are only doing this for email at the moment
   155	
   156	    Users::RecordFieldTestEventWorker
   157	      .perform_async(user_id, AbExperiment::GoalConversionHandler::USER_CREATES_EMAIL_FEED_EVENT_GOAL)
   158	  end
   159	end

1	module Articles
     2	  class HandleSpamWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority
     6	
     7	    def perform(article_id)
     8	      article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     9	      return unless article
    10	
    11	      # Always run spam handling and score update
    12	      Spam::Handler.handle_article!(article: article)
    13	      article.reload.update_score
    14	
    15	      # Only enhance if clickbait score is 0 (not already processed) and score is >= 0 (good quality after spam check)
    16	      return unless article.clickbait_score.zero? && article.score >= 0
    17	
    18	      enhance_article(article)
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    private
    22	
    23	    def enhance_article(article)
    24	      # Create AI client for dependency injection
    25	      ai_client = Ai::Base.new
    26	      enhancer = Ai::ArticleEnhancer.new(article, ai_client: ai_client)
    27	
    28	      # Calculate and update clickbait score
    29	      clickbait_score = enhancer.calculate_clickbait_score
    30	      article.update_column(:clickbait_score, clickbait_score)
    31	
    32	      # Generate and apply tags if article has no tags and meets criteria
    33	      if should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
    34	        suggested_tags = enhancer.generate_tags
    35	        apply_tags_to_article(article, suggested_tags) if suggested_tags.any?
    36	      end
    37	    rescue StandardError => e
    38	      Rails.logger.error("Article enhancement failed for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    def should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
    42	      article.cached_tag_list.blank? &&
    43	        article.score >= 0 &&
    44	        clickbait_score < 0.6
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def apply_tags_to_article(article, tag_names)
    48	      # Validate that all suggested tags exist and are supported
    49	      valid_tags = Tag.supported.where(name: tag_names).pluck(:name)
    50	
    51	      if valid_tags.any?
    52	        # Apply tags to article
    53	        article.tag_list = valid_tags
    54	        article.cached_tag_list = valid_tags.join(", ")
    55	        article.save
    56	
    57	        Rails.logger.info("Applied tags #{valid_tags.join(', ')} to article #{article.id}")
    58	      else
    59	        Rails.logger.warn("No valid tags found from suggestions #{tag_names.join(', ')} for article #{article.id}")
    60	      end
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	end

     2	  class UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     5	
     6	    def perform(article_id)
     7	      article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     8	      
     9	      references = LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: ["Article", nil], referenced_id: article_id)
    10	      
    11	      if article&.organization_id.present?
    12	        references = references.or(LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: "Organization", referenced_id: article.organization_id, tag_name: "org_posts"))
    13	      end
    14	
    15	      references.find_each do |reference|
    16	        record = reference.record
    17	        next unless record
    18	
    19	        original_html = record.processed_html
    20	
    21	        if record.is_a?(Article)
    22	          record.evaluate_and_update_column_from_markdown
    23	          if original_html != record.processed_html
    24	            record.async_bust
    25	          end
    26	        elsif record.respond_to?(:evaluate_markdown, true) && record.respond_to?(:processed_html=)
    27	          record.send(:evaluate_markdown)
    28	          if original_html != record.processed_html
    29	            record.update_column(:processed_html, record.processed_html)
    30	            
    31	            if record.respond_to?(:async_bust)
    32	              record.async_bust
    33	            elsif record.respond_to?(:bust_cache)
    34	              record.send(:bust_cache)
    35	            end
    36	          end
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	    end
    40	  end
    41	end

1	module Articles
     2	  # Maintains the per-article ArticleActivity cache row by applying a single
     3	  # event delta (page view / reaction / comment) instead of re-querying the
     4	  # whole day. If the article has no activity row yet, one is created and a
     5	  # full backfill from raw rows runs — this is the "upsert" referenced in the
     6	  # design. Subsequent events then take the fast append path.
     7	  #
     8	  # Args:
     9	  #   article_id : Integer
    10	  #   event_type : "page_view" | "reaction" | "comment" | nil (nil = full recompute)
    11	  #   action     : "create" | "destroy"
    12	  #   payload    : Hash of attributes carried inline so destroys work even
    13	  #                after the source row is gone. Keys vary by event_type:
    14	  #     page_view: { iso, total, sum_read_seconds, logged_in_count, domain }
    15	  #     reaction:  { iso, category, user_id }
    16	  #     comment:   { iso, score }
    17	  class UpdateArticleActivityWorker
    18	    include Sidekiq::Job
    19	
    20	    # Deltas are non-idempotent — applying the same job twice would
    21	    # double-count (or, on destroy, drive totals negative). retry: false
    22	    # accepts that a transient failure loses one event; the next full
    23	    # recompute (lazy upsert path or manual repair) reconciles drift.
    24	    # We use sidekiq-unique-jobs to ensure at most one job is enqueued/scheduled.
    25	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority,
    26	                    retry: false,
    27	                    lock: :until_executing,
    28	                    on_conflict: :replace
    29	
    30	    class << self
    31	      def perform_async(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
    32	        return if article_id.nil?
    33	
    34	        # Fetch last_aggregated_at, page_views_count, and created_at in a single fast query
    35	        article_data = Article.left_outer_joins(:article_activity)
    36	                              .where(id: article_id)
    37	                              .pluck(:created_at, :page_views_count, "article_activities.last_aggregated_at")
    38	                              .first
    39	        return if article_data.nil?
    40	
    41	        created_at, page_views_count, last_aggregated_at = article_data
    42	
    43	        delay = debounce_delay_for(page_views_count.to_i, created_at)
    44	
    45	        if last_aggregated_at.nil?
    46	          # If never aggregated, run immediately
    47	          super(article_id)
    48	        else
    49	          elapsed = Time.current - last_aggregated_at
    50	          if elapsed >= delay
    51	            # If it has been longer than the delay, run immediately
    52	            super(article_id)
    53	          else
    54	            # Schedule it for the remaining delay time
    55	            # Since lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace is active,
    56	            # this will replace/coalesce any already scheduled job for this article.
    57	            perform_in(delay - elapsed, article_id)
    58	          end
    59	        end
    60	      end
    61	
    62	      def debounce_delay_for(page_views, created_at)
    63	        base_delay = if page_views >= 100_000
    64	                       5.minutes
    65	                     elsif page_views >= 10_000
    66	                       1.minute
    67	                     elsif page_views >= 1_000
    68	                       30.seconds
    69	                     else
    70	                       10.seconds
    71	                     end
    72	
    73	        return base_delay if created_at.nil?
    74	
    75	        # Exponential decay/backoff based on age (up to 30 days to avoid float overflow)
    76	        days_old = [[(Time.current - created_at) / 1.day, 0].max, 30].min
    77	        calculated_delay = base_delay * (1.5**days_old)
    78	
    79	        [calculated_delay, 30.minutes].min
    80	      end
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def perform(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
    84	      return if article_id.nil?
    85	
    86	      activity = ArticleActivity.find_by(article_id: article_id)
    87	
    88	      if activity.nil?
    89	        return unless Article.exists?(id: article_id)
    90	
    91	        activity = ArticleActivity.find_or_create_by!(article_id: article_id)
    92	        activity.recompute_all! if activity.previously_new_record?
    93	        return
    94	      end
    95	
    96	      # Always recompute on execution so legacy queued jobs with delta-style
    97	      # args converge with newer article_id-only jobs. This avoids drift when
    98	      # an older non-idempotent delta job runs after a recompute/coalesced job.
    99	      activity.recompute_all!
   100	    end
   101	  end
   102	end

1	class GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker
     2	  include Sidekiq::Job
     3	  sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     4	
     5	  def perform(article_id)
     6	    article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     7	    return unless article && article.published? && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)
     8	
     9	    # Lightweight representation: Title, Tags, and first 1000 characters of the body
    10	    text_to_embed = <<~TEXT
    11	      Title: #{article.title}
    12	      Tags: #{article.cached_tag_list}
    13	      Summary: #{article.body_markdown.truncate(1000)}
    14	    TEXT
    15	
    16	    client = Ai::Embedding.new(affected_content: article, wrapper: self)
    17	    embedding = client.call(text_to_embed, task_type: "RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT", output_dimensionality: 768)
    18	
    19	    if embedding
    20	      article.update_column(:semantic_embedding, embedding)
    21	      UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(article.user_id, article.id)
    22	      Concepts::ClassifyRecordWorker.perform_async("Article", article.id)
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	end

1	class LiquidEmbedExtractor
     2	  # We implemented a non-rendering parser scanning body_markdown directly to avoid instantiating Liquid tags.
     3	  # This prevents background sync jobs from invoking Pundit auth, HTTP network requests (like UnifiedEmbed fetching OpenGraph data),
     4	  # or halting the extraction entirely if a single tag fails Liquid::Template context validations.
     5	  LIQUID_TAG_REGEX = /\{%\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s+(.*?)\s*%\}/
     6	
     7	  def self.extract(record)
     8	    content = record.respond_to?(:body_markdown) ? record.body_markdown : nil

    12	    stripped_content = content.to_s.gsub(/```.*?```/m, "").gsub(/`.*?`/, "")
    13	
    14	    tags = []
    15	    stripped_content.scan(LIQUID_TAG_REGEX).each do |match|
    16	      tag_name = match[0].downcase
    17	      options = match[1].strip
    18	      url = options.split(" ").first || options
    19	
    20	      ref_type, ref_id = derive_reference(tag_name, url)
    21	
    22	      tags << {
    23	        tag_name: tag_name,
    24	        url: url,
    25	        options: options,
    26	        referenced_type: ref_type,
    27	        referenced_id: ref_id
    28	      }
    29	    end

    31	    tags.uniq { |t| [t[:tag_name], t[:url], t[:options]] }

    34	  def self.derive_reference(tag_name, identifier)
    35	    case tag_name
    36	    when "user"
    37	      user = User.find_by(username: identifier)
    38	      [user&.class&.name, user&.id]
    39	    when "comment"
    40	      comment = Comment.find_by(id_code: identifier)
    41	      [comment&.class&.name, comment&.id]
    42	    when "tag"
    43	      tag = Tag.find_by(name: identifier)
    44	      [tag&.class&.name, tag&.id]
    45	    when "podcast"
    46	      podcast = Podcast.find_by(slug: identifier)
    47	      [podcast&.class&.name, podcast&.id]
    48	    when "organization", "org_posts"
    49	      org = Organization.find_by(slug: identifier)
    50	      [org&.class&.name, org&.id]
    51	    when "embed"
    52	      # If tag is explicitly embed, map internal Article urls without running ActionDispatch logic natively.
    53	      app_domain = Settings::General.app_domain || "localhost:#{URL.dev_port}"
    54	      if identifier.include?(app_domain)
    55	        begin
    56	          path = URI.parse(identifier).path
    57	          if path.present?
    58	            segments = path.split("/")
    59	            if segments.size >= 3
    60	              article = Article.joins(:user).find_by(users: { username: segments[1] }, slug: segments[2])
    61	              return [article.class.name, article.id] if article
    62	            end
    63	          end
    64	        rescue URI::InvalidURIError
    65	          # safely ignore invalid payload URLs dynamically
    66	        end
    67	      end
    68	      [nil, nil]
    69	    else
    70	      [nil, nil]
    71	    end
    72	  end
    73	end

1	class SyncWebpageReferencesWorker
     2	  include Sidekiq::Job
     3	  sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     4	
     5	  def perform(record_class_name, record_id)
     6	    record_class = record_class_name.safe_constantize
     7	    return unless record_class
     8	
     9	    record = record_class.find_by(id: record_id)
    10	    return unless record
    11	
    12	    urls = WebpageExtractor.extract(record)
    13	    
    14	    previous_domain_ids = record.webpage_references.pluck(:linked_domain_id)
    15	    current_domain_ids = []
    16	
    17	    WebpageReference.transaction do
    18	      # 1. Clear out existing references for this record
    19	      record.webpage_references.delete_all
    20	
    21	      # 2. Process and insert new references
    22	      if urls.any?
    23	        references_to_insert = []
    24	        urls.each do |url|
    25	          domain = LinkedDomain.find_or_create_by_url(url)
    26	          next unless domain
    27	
    28	          current_domain_ids << domain.id
    29	
    30	          references_to_insert << {
    31	            record_type: record_class_name,
    32	            record_id: record.id,
    33	            linked_domain_id: domain.id,
    34	            url: url,
    35	            created_at: Time.current,
    36	            updated_at: Time.current
    37	          }
    38	        end
    39	
    40	        WebpageReference.insert_all(references_to_insert) if references_to_insert.any?
    41	      end
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    # Trigger score update for the domains involved
    45	    domain_ids_to_update = (previous_domain_ids + current_domain_ids).uniq
    46	    
    47	    domain_ids_to_update.each do |domain_id|
    48	      LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker.perform_async(domain_id)
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	end

1	class WebpageExtractor
     2	  # Regular expression to find URLs in markdown, avoiding those in code blocks or liquid tags
     3	  # This is a simplified regex; for production, we might want something more robust.
     4	  URL_REGEX = %r{https?://[^\s<>"{}|\\^`\[\]]+}
     5	
     6	  def self.extract(record)
     7	    content = record.respond_to?(:body_markdown) ? record.body_markdown : nil
     8	    return [] if content.blank?
     9	
    10	    # Strip codeblocks and liquid tags to reliably extract active links only
    11	    stripped_content = content.to_s
    12	      .gsub(/```.*?```/m, "") # Fenced code blocks
    13	      .gsub(/`.*?`/, "")     # Inline code
    14	      .gsub(/\{%.*?%\}/, "") # Liquid tags
    15	
    16	    urls = stripped_content.scan(URL_REGEX)
    17	    
    18	    # Clean up URLs (remove trailing punctuation often caught by regex, and markdown parentheses)
    19	    urls.map! { |url| url.gsub(/[.,:;!?)]+$/, "") }
    20	    
    21	    urls.uniq
    22	  end
    23	end
